core_mechanism: field-theoretic renormalization-group treatment of a stochastic many-particle master equation, obtained by second-quantizing (Doi-Peliti mapping) the interacting-population dynamics into a continuum action and computing fluctuation-induced renormalizations of effective parameters near a continuous absorbing-state transition in the directed-percolation universality class
brief_reason: The abstract states the method employs field-theoretic methods based on the Doi-Peliti representation of the master equation to map the action near the absorbing-state transition onto Reggeon field theory (directed-percolation class) and to quantitatively compute fluctuation-induced renormalizations of oscillation frequency, damping, and diffusion coefficients.
